title: How to Handle Unwanted Advice Gracefully
subtitle: 如何优雅应对不请自来的建议

paragraphs.en: People who repeatedly give unwanted advice can be well-meaning and genuinely want to help. Others might do this out of a sense of self-worth around the ability to influence others. One study found that people with a high tendency to seek power were more likely to give advice than those with an opposite tendency.
paragraphs.zh: 反复给出不必要建议的人可能是善意且真心想帮忙。另一些人则可能是出于通过影响他人来获得自我价值感。一项研究发现，权力欲较强的人比倾向相反的人更可能提供建议。

paragraphs.en: Under the guise of unselfishness, people may be driven to give you unwanted advice because it makes them feel powerful or in control. They may not be fully aware of this drive, however. These people may also display a problematic degree of emotional vulnerability, becoming upset very quickly, and taking a long time to calm down.
paragraphs.zh: 在无私的幌子下，人们可能因提供建议让自己感到强大或掌控而驱使如此行事。然而他们可能并未完全意识到这一驱动力。这些人也可能表现出一定程度的情绪脆弱，极易烦躁，且需要很长时间才能平复。

paragraphs.en: It is possible that their emotions were only validated in childhood when they were at their loudest, encouraging them to adopt responses to discomfort that are excessive in most situations. When someone is giving advice in order to make themselves feel more powerful, there is underlying anxiety to their behavior that recipients of the advice tend to notice.
paragraphs.zh: 有可能他们的情绪仅在童年最吵闹时才得到认可，这鼓励他们养成了在大多数情况下对不适反应过度的习惯。当某人为了让自己感觉更强大而提供建议时，其行为背后潜藏着焦虑，而接收者往往能察觉到。

paragraphs.en: It can be tempting in this situation to react harshly to the advice giver and to accuse them of being manipulative, but this approach might produce an undesired result. If the act of giving advice is contributing actively to someone's feelings of self-worth, an outright rejection may be perceived as a threat, activating their fight-or-flight response.
paragraphs.zh: 在这种情况下，人们很容易对建议者做出严厉反应并指责其操纵他人，但这可能适得其反。如果提供建议的行为对某人的自我价值感有积极贡献，断然拒绝可能被视为威胁，从而激活其战斗或逃跑反应。

paragraphs.en: The key is to validate without over-identifying. You can let them know that you have heard them and appreciate where they are coming from without taking on the potentially damaging narrative that you could not have gotten by without their help. You had better say something like, 'Thanks for the idea. I have my own plan for handling this, but I really appreciate your perspective and will take it into consideration.'
paragraphs.zh: 关键在于给予认可但不过度认同。你可以让他们知道你听到了并理解他们的出发点，但不必接受那种“没有他们帮助你就过不去”的潜在伤害性叙事。你最好这样说：“谢谢你的建议。我有自己的处理计划，但真的很感谢你的见解，我会加以考虑。”

paragraphs.en: If you have trouble setting boundaries tactfully, prioritize working on your own ability to self-regulate. As uncomfortable as it may make you continuously receiving unwanted advice, if you can respond with compassion, the situation will likely diffuse much faster. Though it is usually not the intended outcome, many who receive unsolicited advice often feel stressed, offended, or simply annoyed.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你在巧妙设定边界方面有困难，请优先提升自我调节能力。尽管不断收到不请自来的建议令人不适，但若能以同情心回应，局面可能会更快缓和。虽然通常并非有意为之，但许多收到建议的人常感到压力、被冒犯或只是单纯恼火。

words.meaning: adj. 未经请求的，主动提供的

paragraphs.en: Setting a boundary in this regard, if you feel you need one, is perfectly reasonable and something that can bring you increased emotional safety. It is best to set boundaries in a way that takes into account the individual's underlying reasons for doing what they are doing to avoid unnecessary conflict and more stress.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你觉得需要，在此设定边界是完全合理的，且能带给你更强的情绪安全感。最好在设定边界时考虑到对方行为背后的深层原因，以避免不必要的冲突和更多压力。
